title: Intel's AI business helps fuel strongest revenue growth in 15 years
excerpt: Intel beat expectations with its strongest revenue growth in more than 15 years as CEO Lip-Bu Tan credited better execution.
content_html:
Intel looked like a fallen giant. Now it's posting its fastest revenue growth in 15 years.

By Geoff Weiss

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Not long ago, Intel looked like a fallen giant. Now, the chipmaker has posted its "strongest revenue growth in more than fifteen years," CEO Lip-Bu Tan said Thursday.

The company's second-quarter results sent shares up more than 11% in after-hours trading.

The company reported revenue of $16.1 billion, up 25% from a year earlier. It also reported adjusted earnings of $0.42 per share, nearly double Wall Street's expectations.

The company's data center and AI (DCAI) business — an area where it has struggled to keep pace with category leader Nvidia — grew 59% year over year to $6.3 billion.

The results come days after Intel said it was planning layoffs in its data center group as part of an efficiency push.

The results suggest Intel is gaining traction after missing much of the early AI boom. In recent years, it has also been beset by manufacturing delays and lost its spot in the Dow Jones Industrial Average. In 2025, the US government took a 9.9% stake in the chip company.

Today, the company is betting big on its foundry business, which manufactures chips for third-party customers. The strategy helps Intel diversify beyond designing its own chips and brings it closer into competition with manufacturing leader TSMC.

That said, Emarketer senior analyst Jacob Bourne called the foundry business "a work in progress," noting it "lost $2.1 billion and has yet to land the major customers Intel's strategy depends on."

Tan attributed the company's improving performance to better execution — namely, "greater speed, accountability, and customer focus."

Geoff Weiss is a senior reporter on Business Insider’s tech team, where he writes about AI startups and Y Combinator, the intersection of AI and the media industry, and workplace dynamics within top AI labs and chip companies.Previously, Geoff was on the media desk, covering YouTube and Netflix, and themes like the intersection of Hollywood and the creator economy. His work on Netflix’s video podcasting ambitions and Mr Beast’s lessons for Hollywood won second and first prize, respectively, at the 2025 LA Press Club Awards.Prior to joining Business Insider, Geoff was the senior editor of Tubefilter and a staff writer at Entrepreneur. He graduated from New York University with a degree in English Literature.He can be reached at gweiss@businessinsider.com, on Signal @geoffweiss.25, and on LinkedIn. Have a tip? content_html_zh:
英特尔看起来就像一个倒下的巨人。 现在，该公司的收入增长速度创 15 年来最快。

作者：Geoff Weiss 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

不久前，英特尔还像一个倒下的巨人。 首席执行官 Lip-Bu Tan 周四表示，现在，这家芯片制造商公布了“十五年来最强劲的收入增长”。

该公司第二季度业绩推动股价在盘后交易中上涨超过 11%。

该公司报告收入为 161 亿美元，较上年同期增长 25%。 该公司还报告调整后每股收益为 0.42 美元，几乎是华尔街预期的两倍。

该公司的数据中心和人工智能 (DCAI) 业务（该领域一直难以与该领域的领先者 Nvidia 保持同步）同比增长 59%，达到 63 亿美元。

几天前，英特尔表示，作为提高效率的一部分，计划裁员其数据中心部门。

结果表明，英特尔在错过了早期人工智能热潮的大部分时间后，正在获得关注。 近年来，它还受到制造业延误的困扰，并失去了在道琼斯工业平均指数中的地位。 2025年，美国政府收购了该芯片公司9.9%的股份。

如今，该公司正在大力投资其代工业务，该业务为第三方客户生产芯片。 该战略帮助英特尔实现多元化，超越设计自己的芯片，并使其更接近制造领导者台积电的竞争。

尽管如此，Emarketer 高级分析师 Jacob Bourne 称代工业务是“一项正在进行中的工作”，并指出该业务“损失了 21 亿美元，而且尚未获得英特尔战略所依赖的主要客户”。

谭将公司绩效的提高归功于更好的执行力，即“更快的速度、责任感和以客户为中心”。
